description_long: This object consists of two irregularly shaped stones with rough, angular surfaces and a natural, weathered appearance. The stones display a mix of gray, white, and yellowish hues, with some areas showing subtle greenish moss or lichen growth. Their surfaces are uneven, featuring jagged edges, crevices, and a granular texture typical of natural rock. The coloration is mottled, with patches of lighter and darker tones suggesting mineral deposits and exposure to the elements. The stones appear solid and heavy, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. These stones would commonly be found as part of urban landscaping, serving as natural barriers or decorative elements.
description: Two rough, irregular stones with mottled gray, white, and yellow surfaces, showing signs of weathering and natural mineral deposits.
description_view_0: Front view shows both stones side by side, highlighting their angular shapes and the mottled gray and yellow coloration.
description_view_1: Side view of one stone, emphasizing its vertical height and the rough, jagged surface with visible yellowish patches.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the broader faces of both stones, with more pronounced gray and greenish areas, and less yellow.
description_view_3: Opposite side view of a single stone, showing a sloped surface with a mix of gray and yellow, and a more compact profile.
attribute_placement: set as standalone features on soil or gravel
attribute_pattern: Mottled coloration with irregular patches of gray, white, yellow, and some green, with no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough with visible lichen and mineral staining, indicating prolonged exposure to outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, and vegetation, often partially surrounded by grass or low plants.
